tasks.title: Worktree Package
tasks.description:
## Context
Littlefactory needs to detect and manage git worktrees to enable parallel implementation of multiple openspec changes. This package provides the core git worktree operations: detecting worktree-enabled repos, listing existing worktrees, creating new ones, and validating preconditions.

## Scope
- Detect common git directory via `git rev-parse --git-common-dir`
- Check if worktrees exist in the repository
- List all worktrees by parsing `git worktree list`
- Create new worktrees with `git worktree add`
- Validate clean working tree before worktree creation
- Check if a worktree already exists for a given branch

## OpenSpec checklist (reference)
- [ ] 1.1 Create `internal/worktree/detect.go` with `GetCommonDir()` function using `git rev-parse --git-common-dir`
- [ ] 1.2 Add `HasWorktrees()` function that checks if `<common-dir>/worktrees` exists and is non-empty
- [ ] 1.3 Create `internal/worktree/list.go` with `List()` function that parses `git worktree list` output
- [ ] 1.4 Create `internal/worktree/create.go` with `Create(branchName, worktreesDir)` function
- [ ] 1.5 Add `IsClean()` function that checks `git status --porcelain` for uncommitted changes
- [ ] 1.6 Add `WorktreeExists(branchName)` function to check if a worktree for a branch already exists
- [ ] 1.7 Write tests for worktree package

The task list can be found at openspec/changes/worktree-support/tasks.md

## Implementation plan (suggested chunks)
1. Create `internal/worktree/` package directory
2. Implement `detect.go` with `GetCommonDir()` and `HasWorktrees()` functions
3. Implement `list.go` with `List()` function that parses worktree list output into structs
4. Implement `create.go` with `Create()`, `IsClean()`, and `WorktreeExists()` functions
5. Write comprehensive tests covering all functions

## Acceptance criteria
- Build passes with `go build ./...`
- Tests pass with `go test ./internal/worktree/...`
- `GetCommonDir()` correctly returns git common directory path
- `HasWorktrees()` detects existing worktrees
- `List()` parses worktree list into structured data
- `Create()` successfully creates worktrees with correct branch
- `IsClean()` correctly detects uncommitted changes
- `WorktreeExists()` finds existing worktrees by branch name
- All checklist items above are implemented and verified
- The task list at openspec/changes/worktree-support/tasks.md is updated

## Key references
- proposal.md (section: What Changes)
- design.md (section: Decisions, Package Structure)
- specs/worktree-creation/spec.md (section: Worktree detection via git common dir)

tasks.title: Config Updates
tasks.description:
## Context
The Factoryfile configuration needs a new `worktrees_dir` option so users can customize where git worktrees are created. By default, worktrees are created as siblings to the repository (git's default behavior), but some users may prefer a dedicated worktrees directory.

## Scope
- Add `WorktreesDir` field to config struct
- Parse `worktrees_dir` from Factoryfile YAML
- Set sensible default (`..` for sibling directories)
- Validate configuration

## OpenSpec checklist (reference)
- [ ] 2.1 Add `WorktreesDir` field to config struct in `internal/config/config.go`
- [ ] 2.2 Update config parsing to read `worktrees_dir` from Factoryfile
- [ ] 2.3 Set default value for `WorktreesDir` to `..` (sibling to repo)
- [ ] 2.4 Write tests for worktrees_dir config parsing

The task list can be found at openspec/changes/worktree-support/tasks.md

## Implementation plan (suggested chunks)
1. Add `WorktreesDir string` field to Config struct
2. Update YAML struct tags for parsing
3. Set default in config loading logic
4. Add tests for parsing and defaults

## Acceptance criteria
- Build passes with `go build ./...`
- Tests pass with `go test ./internal/config/...`
- `worktrees_dir` is correctly parsed from Factoryfile
- Missing `worktrees_dir` defaults to `..`
- All checklist items above are implemented and verified
- The task list at openspec/changes/worktree-support/tasks.md is updated

## Key references
- proposal.md (section: What Changes)
- design.md (section: Decisions)
- specs/worktree-config/spec.md (section: Worktrees directory configuration)
- specs/config-management/spec.md (section: Worktrees directory configuration)

tasks.title: Run Command Changes
tasks.description:
## Context
The `run` command needs new flags to support worktree-based workflows. The `--change` flag specifies which openspec change's tasks.json to use, and `--worktree` flag triggers worktree creation for isolated implementation.

## Scope
- Add `--change` / `-c` flag for specifying task source
- Add `--worktree` / `-w` flag for worktree creation
- Validate flag combinations and preconditions
- Integrate with worktree package for creation
- Pass configuration to driver

## OpenSpec checklist (reference)
- [ ] 3.1 Add `--change` / `-c` flag to run command
- [ ] 3.2 Add `--worktree` / `-w` flag to run command
- [ ] 3.3 Validate that `-w` requires `-c` flag
- [ ] 3.4 Implement change validation (check `openspec/changes/<name>/tasks.json` exists)
- [ ] 3.5 Implement worktree creation flow with clean-state and exists checks
- [ ] 3.6 Pass change name and worktree path to driver
- [ ] 3.7 Write tests for run command flags

The task list can be found at openspec/changes/worktree-support/tasks.md

## Implementation plan (suggested chunks)
1. Add `--change` and `--worktree` flags to cobra command
2. Implement validation: `-w` requires `-c`, change must exist
3. Add worktree precondition checks (clean, not exists) when `-w` is set
4. Call worktree.Create() when `-w` is set
5. Update driver initialization with new parameters
6. Write tests for all flag combinations and error cases

## Acceptance criteria
- Build passes with `go build ./...`
- `littlefactory run claude -c feature-a` uses correct task source
- `littlefactory run claude -w` errors requiring `-c`
- `littlefactory run claude -c feature-a -w` creates worktree and runs
- Dirty working tree with `-w` produces clear error
- Existing worktree with `-w` produces clear error
- All checklist items above are implemented and verified
- The task list at openspec/changes/worktree-support/tasks.md is updated

## Key references
- proposal.md (section: What Changes)
- design.md (section: Decisions)
- specs/change-flag/spec.md (section: Change flag on run command)
- specs/worktree-creation/spec.md (section: Worktree flag on run command)

tasks.title: Driver Updates
tasks.description:
## Context
The driver orchestrates the agent loop and needs to support running with a specific openspec change as the task source, and optionally in a worktree directory. This enables the core parallel workflow where each change runs in its own workspace.

## Scope
- Accept change name to determine task source path
- Accept worktree path to change working directory
- Resolve task source from `openspec/changes/<name>/tasks.json`
- Switch to worktree directory before running agent

## OpenSpec checklist (reference)
- [ ] 4.1 Add `ChangeName` option to driver configuration
- [ ] 4.2 Add `WorktreePath` option to driver configuration
- [ ] 4.3 Update task source path resolution based on change name
- [ ] 4.4 Implement workspace directory switching when worktree path is set
- [ ] 4.5 Write tests for driver with change and worktree options

The task list can be found at openspec/changes/worktree-support/tasks.md

## Implementation plan (suggested chunks)
1. Add `ChangeName` and `WorktreePath` fields to driver options/config
2. Update task source resolution: if ChangeName set, use `openspec/changes/<name>/tasks.json`
3. Implement directory switching when WorktreePath is set
4. Ensure proper cleanup/restoration of working directory
5. Write tests for various combinations

## Acceptance criteria
- Build passes with `go build ./...`
- Tests pass with `go test ./internal/driver/...`
- Driver uses correct task source when ChangeName is set
- Driver runs in worktree directory when WorktreePath is set
- Default behavior (no change/worktree) unchanged
- All checklist items above are implemented and verified
- The task list at openspec/changes/worktree-support/tasks.md is updated

## Key references
- proposal.md (section: What Changes)
- design.md (section: Package Structure)
- specs/loop-driver/spec.md (section: Driver accepts change name parameter)

tasks.title: Status Command
tasks.description:
## Context
A new `status` command provides visibility into task progress across changes. This is essential for monitoring parallel implementations and knowing when a change is ready for review.

## Scope
- New `status` subcommand
- Show progress for current directory or specific change
- Discover and show all worktrees with `--all`
- Detailed task list with `--verbose`
- Summary format: `<name>: X/Y done`

## OpenSpec checklist (reference)
- [ ] 5.1 Create new `status` subcommand in `cmd/littlefactory/`
- [ ] 5.2 Add `--change` / `-c` flag to status command
- [ ] 5.3 Add `--all` flag to status command
- [ ] 5.4 Add `--verbose` flag for detailed task list
- [ ] 5.5 Implement status summary format: `<name>: X/Y done`
- [ ] 5.6 Implement worktree discovery for `--all` flag using `git worktree list`
- [ ] 5.7 Write tests for status command

The task list can be found at openspec/changes/worktree-support/tasks.md

## Implementation plan (suggested chunks)
1. Create `cmd/littlefactory/status.go` with cobra command
2. Add flags: `--change`, `--all`, `--verbose`
3. Implement single-change status reading tasks.json and counting
4. Implement `--all` using worktree.List() to discover all worktrees
5. Implement `--verbose` to show individual task status
6. Format output clearly and consistently
7. Write tests for all flag combinations

## Acceptance criteria
- Build passes with `go build ./...`
- `littlefactory status` shows status for current directory
- `littlefactory status -c feature-a` shows specific change status
- `littlefactory status --all` lists all worktrees with their status
- `littlefactory status -c feature-a --verbose` shows detailed task list
- Output format is clear: `feature-a: 3/7 done`
- All checklist items above are implemented and verified
- The task list at openspec/changes/worktree-support/tasks.md is updated

## Key references
- proposal.md (section: What Changes)
- design.md (section: Goals)
- specs/status-command/spec.md (section: Status command shows task progress)
